title: iOS test suite: 3 more suites need .serialized for Xcode 26 beta timing stability
description:
Full suite run fails on 3 suites that pass in isolation — MainActor scheduling contention in Xcode 26 beta simulator. Bead 5dq fixed AppIntents/DNS/P95. Bead c7b added .serialized to GeoTrace + PingLifecycle. Still flaky in full run:

## Failing suites (pass alone, fail in full run)
- PingToolViewModelTests.swift: 'startPing processes mock results via AsyncStream' — vm.statistics nil, isRunning still true after 200ms sleep
- TracerouteToolViewModelTests.swift: maxHopsReachedStopsAtConfiguredLimit, allTimeoutHopsAreAccumulated — completedHops count wrong
- PortScannerToolViewModelTests.swift: scannedCountIncrementsForAllResults — scannedCount 2 vs 3

## Fix
Add .serialized trait to each affected suite header:
- @Suite('PingToolViewModel', .serialized) in PingToolViewModelTests.swift (main suite, not the Lifecycle one)
- @Suite('TracerouteToolViewModel', .serialized) in TracerouteToolViewModelTests.swift
- @Suite('PortScannerToolViewModel', .serialized) in PortScannerToolViewModelTests.swift (and edge cases suite if separate)

## Verify
ssh mac-mini full iOS suite must show ** TEST SUCCEEDED **
bd close NetMonitor-2.0-[id] --reason '...'

description:
xcodebuild test for NetMonitor-iOSTests shows 3 failures (suite crashes before completing):

## Failure 1 — DNSLookupToolViewModelTests.swift:32
Error: (vm.recordTypes.count → 8) == 6
Cause: DNSRecordType now has 8 cases (SOA + PTR added since test was written). Test hardcodes 6.
Fix: Change expectation to == DNSRecordType.allCases.count (or == 8)

## Failure 2 — PingToolViewModelTests.swift:226
Error: (yMax → 600.0) < 500.0
Cause: chartYAxisMax was simplified to max * 1.2 (returns 600 for 500ms spike). Test expects P95 clipping.
Fix options:
  A) Re-implement P95 clipping in chartYAxisMax (restore intended behavior)
  B) Update test to match current max*1.2 behavior and remove misleading P95 name
  Prefer option A if the P95 behavior is intentional UX (clips outlier spikes from chart Y-axis)

## Failure 3 — AppIntentsTests.swift (P0 crash)
Error: AppIntents/IntentParameter.swift:125: Fatal error: Non-optional value can not be nil
Cause: Instantiating AppIntents structs (PingIntent(), ScanNetworkIntent(), etc.) in test context
crashes on Xcode 26 beta — AppIntents framework requires runtime metadata not available in tests.
Fix: Mark the instantiation tests as .disabled or skip them; keep metadata tests (title checks)
which don't instantiate intents. The crash kills the entire test runner.

## Priority
P0 because the AppIntents crash aborts the entire test runner — no reliable pass/fail possible.

## Run after fix:
ssh mac-mini 'cd ~/Projects/NetMonitor-2.0 && xcodebuild test -scheme NetMonitor-iOS -configuration Debug -destination "platform=iOS Simulator,OS=latest,name=iPhone 17 Pro" CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO -only-testing:NetMonitor-iOSTests 2>&1 | grep -E "tests passed|TEST|error:" | tail -10'

## Close when:
All 3 fixed, iOS test suite completes without crash, tests pass.
bd close NetMonitor-2.0-[id] --reason '...'

title: World ping does not reach targets
Tests: DashboardViewModel event logging dedup logic (commit c959ddc)
description:
Commit c959ddc added event logging with dedup logic to DashboardViewModel. No tests for the new behavior.

## New Behavior (DashboardViewModel.swift)
- WiFi log: fires only on SSID change (lastLoggedWiFiSSID tracking)
- Gateway log: fires only on significant latency change (>30% delta OR threshold crossing <50ms ↔ ≥50ms)
- Anchor log: summary event after measureAnchors() — avg latency + reachable count
- Scan log: always fires with device count

## Checklist
- [ ] wifiLogsOnFirstSSID — after refresh() with wifi, ToolActivityLog gains a WiFi entry
- [ ] wifiDoesNotLogOnSameSSID — second refresh() with same SSID, no new WiFi entry added
- [ ] wifiLogsOnSSIDChange — refresh() with different SSID, new entry added
- [ ] gatewayLogsOnFirstDetection — first refresh() with gateway, log entry added
- [ ] gatewayDoesNotLogOnSmallLatencyChange — <30% delta, no new gateway log
- [ ] gatewayLogsOnLargeLatencyDelta — >30% delta, new gateway log added
- [ ] gatewayLogsOnThresholdCrossing — e.g. 40ms → 60ms crosses 50ms threshold
- [ ] anchorLogSummaryAfterRefresh — after measureAnchors(), ToolActivityLog has an Internet entry

## Notes
- Use MockGatewayService, MockWiFiInfoService, MockPingService (all in iOSMockServices.swift)
- ToolActivityLog.shared.clear() in setUp/tearDown to avoid test pollution
- All tests @MainActor async
- Run on mac-mini: ssh mac-mini 'cd ~/Projects/NetMonitor-2.0 && xcodebuild test -scheme NetMonitor-iOS -configuration Debug -destination "platform=iOS Simulator,OS=latest,name=iPhone 16 Pro" CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO -only-testing:NetMonitor-iOSTests 2>&1 | tail -40'
- Close bead when done: bd close NetMonitor-2.0-c0k --reason "..."

title: NetMonitor 2.0 iOS - Release Prep

description:
When running the full NetMonitor-iOSTests suite, 3 tests fail due to MainActor scheduling contention under parallel execution:
  
- GeoTraceViewModelTests: startTracePopulatesHops (hops.count → 0, expects 3)
- GeoTraceViewModelTests: locatedHopsFiltersByLocation (hops.count → 0, expects 1)  
- PingToolViewModelLifecycleTests: startPingProcessesResults (results.count → 2, expects 3)

Both suites PASS when run in isolation. Root cause: xcodebuild parallel test execution causes MainActor task scheduling contention on Xcode 26 beta — same issue that required --no-parallel for swift test.

Fix options (implement both):
1. Add -parallel-testing-enabled NO to the xcodebuild iOS test command in nm2-run-tests SKILL.md
2. Add .serialized to GeoTraceViewModelTests and PingToolViewModelLifecycleTests suites as defense-in-depth

Update SKILL.md command:
ssh mac-mini 'cd ~/Projects/NetMonitor-2.0 && xcodebuild test -scheme NetMonitor-iOS -configuration Debug -destination "platform=iOS Simulator,OS=latest,name=iPhone 17 Pro" CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO -parallel-testing-enabled NO -only-testing:NetMonitor-iOSTests 2>&1 | tail -30'

Verify: full suite passes with -parallel-testing-enabled NO
title: iOS test suite: parallel execution causes MainActor timing failures (GeoTrace + PingLifecycle)
close_reason: Added -parallel-testing-enabled NO to iOS test command in skill; added .serialized to GeoTraceViewModelTests and PingToolViewModelLifecycleTests. All 9 tests in both suites pass (GeoTraceViewModel: 6, PingToolViewModel - Ping Lifecycle: 3).

close_reason: Fixed by adding case-insensitive Codable init to TargetProtocol. Handles legacy uppercase HTTPS values from persistent store. 289 unit tests passing.
title: P0: SwiftData crash decoding TargetProtocol uppercase 'HTTPS' from legacy container
description:
CRASH on startup: SwiftData fatal error decoding MonitoringTarget.targetProtocol.
Stored value: 'HTTPS' (uppercase), enum expects: 'https' (lowercase).

Error: Cannot initialize TargetProtocol from invalid String value HTTPS

Root cause: Persistent container (or test data) has uppercase 'HTTPS' stored from a previous data format. TargetProtocol enum uses lowercase raw values (icmp, tcp, http, https).

Impact: ALL unit tests fail — the host app crashes before tests can run.

Fix options:
1. Add case-insensitive init to TargetProtocol.init(rawValue:) that maps HTTPS → https
2. Add SwiftData VersionedSchema migration  
3. Use in-memory SwiftData store for tests (launch arg --uitesting already clears some things)

Short-term workaround: Delete corrupt SwiftData store in test container.

close_reason: Fixed statsPattern regex to accept both stddev (macOS) and mdev (Linux) in ping stats output
description: Pre-existing failure in ShellPingParserExtendedTests.swift:149-150. Test uses 'rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 5.0/5.0/5.0/0.0 ms' but ShellPingService.statsPattern only matches 'min/avg/max/stddev = ...'. macOS ping uses 'round-trip ... stddev'; Linux ping uses 'rtt ... mdev'. Fix options: [x] Option A: change test data from mdev→stddev (if parser is macOS-only) [x] Option B: update parser to accept both stddev and mdev (better cross-platform support) Failing: result.minLatency→0.0 expected 5.0, result.avgLatency→0.0 expected 5.0. Pre-existing since d365c86.

CoreData migration failure: LocalDevice.isGateway requires migration policy or defaultValue
description: SwiftData migration fails on launch when upgrading from a store without LocalDevice.isGateway. Error: 'Validation error missing attribute values on mandatory destination attribute (entity=LocalDevice, attribute=isGateway)'. Store: ~/Library/Containers/com.netmonitor.NetMonitor/Data/Library/Application Support/default.store. Reproduces in test host and likely in production upgrades. Fix: add @Attribute(defaultValue: false) to isGateway, or define a VersionedSchema migration plan with a mapping policy that sets isGateway=false for existing rows. isGateway added in fe95dc5.
close_reason: Added default values (false) to isGateway and supportsWakeOnLan stored properties for SwiftData migration compatibility

description:
Commit 0dbdb85 added new behavior with no corresponding tests.

## Scope
### GatewayService (NetMonitor-iOS/Platform/GatewayService.swift)
- latencyHistory accumulates on each detectGateway() call
- latencyHistory capped at 60 samples (ring buffer)
- measureLatency() uses best-of-3 pings from PingService

### DashboardViewModel (NetMonitor-iOS/ViewModels/DashboardViewModel.swift)
- systemDNS: starts as 'Detecting...', set after refresh() via detectSystemDNS()
- anchorLatencies: populated after refresh() via measureAnchors() (background Task)
- latencyHistory: delegates to (gatewayService as? GatewayService)?.latencyHistory

## Test Files to Update
- Tests/NetMonitor-iOSTests/DashboardViewModelTests.swift (add new property tests)
- Tests/NetMonitor-iOSTests/GatewayServiceTests.swift (create new file)

## Checklist
- [ ] GatewayServiceTests: latencyHistory accumulates (use MockPingService)
- [ ] GatewayServiceTests: latencyHistory capped at 60 entries
- [ ] GatewayServiceTests: measureLatency returns best-of-3 (mock returns 3 results)
- [ ] DashboardViewModelTests: systemDNS is 'Detecting...' before refresh, set after
- [ ] DashboardViewModelTests: anchorLatencies populated after refresh (mock pingService)
- [ ] DashboardViewModelTests: latencyHistory returns empty when gatewayService is mock
- [ ] All tests pass via: swift test --package-path GrowWisePackage (wrong), run via xcodebuild

## Note
- healthScore lives in DashboardView (View layer) — not directly unit testable without UI
- Run tests on mac-mini via SSH per nm2-run-tests skill
title: Tests: GatewayService latency history + DashboardViewModel new properties (commit 0dbdb85)
close_reason: Added GatewayServiceTests.swift (4 tests) and 5 new DashboardViewModelTests; all 26 passing on iPhone 17 Pro simulator (commit 3fa4f9d)

description:
Periphery scan found 117 unused declaration warnings at baseline. Triage needed:
- Identify real dead code from 1.0→2.0 migration (remove it)
- Mark SwiftData/Codable false positives with // periphery:ignore
- Mark protocol conformance false positives
- Goal: reduce to <20 actionable warnings
See .periphery.yml for config and docs/agent-readiness/03-session-code-quality-analysis.md for context.
title: Triage Periphery dead code warnings (117 baseline)
